edges.description: Failed left-right organizer signaling disrupts conserved downstream laterality pathways that pattern asymmetric organ development.
Abnormal left-right patterning can specify bilateral right-sided atrial morphology.
Abnormal left-right patterning can specify bilateral left-sided atrial morphology.
Disturbed left-right cardiac patterning commonly disrupts atrioventricular septation.
Abnormal visceral laterality can disrupt intestinal rotation and fixation.
Abnormal atrial isomerism, especially left isomerism, can disrupt cardiac conduction.
Right isomerism/asplenia spectrum reflects abnormal spleen laterality development.
Left isomerism/polysplenia spectrum reflects abnormal spleen laterality development.

nodes.description: Right-isomerism heterotaxy is often aligned with the asplenia spectrum, creating infection-prevention implications.

evidence.snippet: Left (polysplenia syndrome) or right (asplenia syndrome) isomerism are usually observed.

nodes.description: Atrioventricular septal defects are among the most frequent major cardiac malformations across both left- and right-isomerism presentations of fetal heterotaxy.

evidence.reference_title: Outcome of prenatally diagnosed fetal heterotaxy: systematic review and meta-analysis.
evidence.snippet: Atrioventricular septal defect was the most common associated major cardiac anomaly found both in fetuses with LAI (pooled proportion (PP), 59.3% (95% CI, 44.0-73.7%)), with obstructive lesions of the right outflow tract occurring in 35.5% of these cases, and in fetuses with RAI (PP, 72.9% (95% CI, 60.4-83.7%)).

nodes.description: Fetal arrhythmias, especially atrioventricular block in left atrial isomerism, are an established rhythm complication within the heterotaxy spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Outcome of prenatally diagnosed fetal heterotaxy: systematic review and meta-analysis.
evidence.snippet: Fetal arrhythmias occurred in 36.7% (95% CI, 26.9-47.2%) of cases with LAI and were mainly represented by complete atrioventricular block, while this finding was uncommon in cases with RAI (PP, 1.3% (95% CI, 0.2-3.2%)).

nodes.description: Intestinal malrotation is a recurrent extracardiac manifestation of heterotaxy and requires deliberate anatomic assessment because it can drive volvulus risk and surgical decision-making.

evidence.reference_title: Outcome of prenatally diagnosed fetal heterotaxy: systematic review and meta-analysis.
evidence.snippet: while intestinal malrotation was detected in 14.2% (95% CI, 2.5-33.1%) of LAI and 27.1% (95% CI, 7.9-52.0%) of RAI cases.

nodes.description: The other major heterotaxy subgroup is left atrial appendage isomerism, often aligned clinically with the polysplenia spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Cardiac and Non-Cardiac Abnormalities in Heterotaxy Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: These subgroups are - 1) Isomerism of right atrial appendage (asplenia syndrome); 2) Isomerism of left atrial appendage (polysplenia syndrome);

nodes.description: Heterotaxy arises when motile cilia-dependent left-right organizer function fails during early embryogenesis. Defective cilium movement perturbs the earliest symmetry-breaking events and prevents robust specification of the left-right axis.

evidence.reference_title: Disorders of left-right asymmetry: heterotaxy and situs inversus.
evidence.snippet: Cilia function is critical to the development of proper organ laterality.

evidence.reference_title: Understanding laterality disorders and the left-right organizer: Insights from zebrafish.
evidence.snippet: Mutations identified in patients with laterality disorders implicate motile cilia in establishing LR asymmetry.

nodes.description: Downstream of cilia-driven symmetry breaking, abnormal nodal signaling and impaired PITX2 activation disrupt left-right pattern formation across the developing heart and abdominal viscera. This laterality-patterning failure yields the cardio-visceral organ discordance that defines visceral heterotaxy.

evidence.snippet: This pathway involves initial asymmetric activation of a nodal signaling cascade at the embryonic node, followed by its propagation to the left lateral plate mesoderm and activation of left-sided expression of the Pitx2 transcription factor specifying visceral organ asymmetry.
Analysis of model organisms and gene expression during early development suggests ZIC3-related heterotaxy occurs due to defects at the earliest stage of left-right axis formation.

nodes.description: Left-isomerism heterotaxy is often aligned with the polysplenia spectrum.

evidence.snippet: Left (polysplenia syndrome) or right (asplenia syndrome) isomerism are usually observed.

nodes.description: One major heterotaxy subgroup is right atrial appendage isomerism, often aligned clinically with the asplenia spectrum and severe early cardiac disease.

evidence.reference_title: Cardiac and Non-Cardiac Abnormalities in Heterotaxy Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: These subgroups are - 1) Isomerism of right atrial appendage (asplenia syndrome); 2) Isomerism of left atrial appendage (polysplenia syndrome);
